action.reasoning: Observation: Iteration 20 achieved a new best single-model test AUC of 0.7605 (m2.pt, val_auc=0.7625). We have ~10 minutes and 10 iterations remaining. The three models trained in iteration 20 (m0, m1, m2) all have strong validation AUCs (0.7604, 0.7615, 0.7625) and identical compatible architectures. Analysis: Since the models are already trained and present in the workspace, we can form a 3-model ensemble by copying them into a clean directory and evaluating with the existing predict.py, which averages predictions across all loadable checkpoints. This costs almost no training time and ensembles typically improve AUC by reducing variance. Decision: Create a clean ensemble directory with m0.pt, m1.pt, m2.pt and submit it for evaluation immediately.
action.command: mkdir -p ensemble_best && cp m0.pt m1.pt m2.pt ensemble_best/ && ls ensemble_best/
